pattern: Counting values up to a huge bound is a digit-DP problem: build the number one digit at a time, tracking the previous digit and whether you are still pinned to the bound's own digits, and get [low, high] by subtracting the count up to low-1 from the count up to high.
hints: The bound can have up to 100 digits, far past normal integer range, so this has to be solved digit by digit rather than by looping over numbers.

Count 'stepping numbers from 1 up to N' as its own helper: build the number left to right, remembering only the previous digit placed and whether every digit so far has matched N's own digits exactly (which caps what the next digit can be).

The answer for [low, high] is countUpTo(high) minus countUpTo(low - 1); since low and high are given as strings, subtracting one means a manual borrow over the digit string rather than ordinary integer subtraction.

edge_inputs.description: every single-digit number is trivially a stepping number
low equals high on a genuine stepping number
low equals high on a non-stepping number
the range crosses from one digit-length to the next
a narrow range containing a repeated-digit number that must not be counted
a large-magnitude but numerically narrow range
a 50-digit alternating stepping number as both bounds, exercising the full digit-DP depth

stress.description: random same-length low/high pairs of varying digit counts, low <= high
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    length = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 60)))
    def rand_digits(n):
        first = str(rng.randint(1, 9))
        rest = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n - 1))
        return first + rest
    a = rand_digits(length)
    b = rand_digits(length)
    lo, hi = (a, b) if a <= b else (b, a)
    return [lo, hi]
